item.dc:creator: Sara G. Miller
item.description: If additional Americans contract Ebola and need medical care, they will be sent to Europe rather than brought home to the U.S., senior administration officials said.

media:content.media:text: Workers load World Health Organization emergency supplies onto a United Nations plane
media:content.media:description: Workers load World Health Organization emergency supplies onto a United Nations plane in Nairobi, Kenya, on May 20.

item.title: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say

item.description: Los Angeles mayoral candidate and former reality TV star Spencer Pratt sits down with NBC News' Tom Llamas to discuss his campaign, in which he accuses Mayor Karen Bass and Governor Gavin Newsom of "criminal negligence" over their handling of the devastating L.A. wildfires.

media:content.media:title: L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt accuses Karen Bass of 'criminal negligence'
media:content.media:description: Los Angeles mayoral candidate and former reality TV star Spencer Pratt sits down with NBC News' Tom Llamas to discuss his campaign, in which he accuses Mayor Karen Bass and Governor Gavin Newsom of "criminal negligence" over their handling of the devastating L.A. wildfires.

item.title: L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt accuses Karen Bass of 'criminal negligence'

item.dc:creator: Peter Nicholas
item.description: 13 farmers across Iowa said they’re struggling economically, but 11 voted for Trump in the past and still largely back him, hoping “he follows through” on his promises.

media:content.media:text: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer, at his home in Galt, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Stu Swanson’s Hagie crop sprayer.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Newly planted corn grows in a field on Stu Swanson’s farm in Galt, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: The Gold-Eagle Cooperative feed mill and grain facility, where Stu Swanson sells corn from his farm, is visible from his property in Galt.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Image: President Trump Speaks To Farmers At The White House
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Mark Mueller.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Steve Rehder and Corey, Doug and Don Winterfeld.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Jason Orr
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Stu Swanson's farm in Galt, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: Travis, Arda and Loren Van Regenmorter on their family farm in Sioux Center, Iowa.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

media:content.media:text: A photo in Stu Swanson’s home of his parents, Florine and Ron Swanson, who farmed the same land.
media:content.media:description: Stu Swanson, a corn and soybean farmer in Galt, Iowa, voted for Donald Trump twice but wrote in Nikki Haley's name in 2024.

item.title: Farmers in Iowa are struggling in Trump’s economy, but many say they still support him

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Adam Reiss
item.description: CBS News editor Bari Weiss hired a new executive producer at “60 Minutes,” the network’s flagship newsmagazine: Nick Bilton, a former New York Times tech columnist.

media:content.media:text: Nick Bilton.
media:content.media:description: Nick Bilton in Beverly Hills, Calif., in 2017.

media:content.media:text: Bari Weiss.
media:content.media:description: Nick Bilton in Beverly Hills, Calif., in 2017.

item.title: In major shake-up, CBS replaces ‘60 Minutes’ executive producer with tech journalist

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
item.description: Soccer fans headed to Houston for the World Cup this summer can expect an out-of-this-world experience as the city combines its connection to the stars while roping in some of the best of what the city has to offer by keeping two feet on the ground.

media:content.media:text: The exterior of NRG Stadium with World Cup signage
media:content.media:description: NRG Stadium, which will be called Houston Stadium during the World Cup, on Wednesday.

media:content.media:text: An interior view of NRG Stadium, the astroturf is being sprayed with a pink substance
media:content.media:description: NRG Stadium, which will be called Houston Stadium during the World Cup, on Wednesday.

item.title: Houston planning out-of-this-world experience for World Cup fans this summer

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins
Tom Winter
Jonathan Dienst
item.description: Former NBA player Terry Rozier was hit with new federal charges of sports bribery and honest services wire fraud conspiracy in a massive sports betting investigation that has rocked the league.

media:content.media:text: Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier
media:content.media:description: Terry Rozier arrives at U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of New York last year.

item.title: Feds say ex-NBA player Terry Rozier accepted $100K bribe in sports betting scheme

item.dc:creator: Angela Yang
Jared Perlo
item.description: As companies race to weave AI into nearly every industry, some college students are responding with open hostility

media:content.media:text: NBC News spoke with seven students from universities across the country who described seeing their peers rely on AI to cut corners in school, sometimes at the encouragement of faculty members.
media:content.media:description: NBC News spoke with seven students from universities across the country who described seeing their peers rely on AI to cut corners in school, sometimes at the encouragement of faculty members.

media:content.media:text: NBC News spoke with seven students from universities across the country who described seeing their peers rely on AI to cut corners in school, sometimes at the encouragement of faculty members.
media:content.media:description: NBC News spoke with seven students from universities across the country who described seeing their peers rely on AI to cut corners in school, sometimes at the encouragement of faculty members.

item.title: Student commencement boos are a sign of wider AI woes

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
Gabe Gutierrez
item.description: The Treasury Department is preparing to print $250 bills with President Donald Trump’s face on them and is just waiting for Congress’ green light, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday

media:content.media:text: Scott Bessen.
media:content.media:description: Scott Bessent displays an article on the proposed $250 banknote featuring an image of Donald Trump during a news conference in the James S. Brady Press Briefing Room of the White House on May 28, 2026.

item.title: Trump administration prepares for proposed $250 bill with the president’s face on it

item.dc:creator: Doha Madani
Corky Siemaszko
item.description: Teenagers behaving badly in groups is hardly a new thing, experts said. But social media has made it easier to rapidly mobilize this generation of teenagers, who may be craving social interaction and looking for an outlet after having spent their formative years isolated during the Covid pandemic.

media:content.media:text: A still from a video showing 'Teen takeovers' in Chicago on Memorial Day, on May 26, 2026.
media:content.media:description: A still from a video showing 'Teen takeovers' in Chicago on Memorial Day, on May 26, 2026.

item.title: Teen ‘takeovers’ push cities to take action as experts see echoes of the Covid pandemic

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Former reality TV star and Los Angeles mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt rebuffed a potential endorsement from President Donald Trump, telling NBC News on Thursday, “I don’t need anyone’s endorsement but mothers’.

media:content.media:text: Spencer Pratt.
media:content.media:description: Former reality TV star Spencer Pratt is running for mayor in Los Angeles after criticizing the government's response to wildfires.

item.title: Spencer Pratt says he doesn’t need ‘anyone’s endorsement’ after Trump backs L.A. mayor bid

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: An unknown number of people were killed and at least four people were hospitalized after a gas explosion destroyed a Dallas apartment complex Thursday afternoon, city officials said

media:content.media:text: A natural gas explosion destroyed a residential building, injuring at least one person, in Dallas, Texas, on Thursday, 28 May 2026.
media:content.media:description: A natural gas explosion destroyed a residential building, injuring at least one person, in Dallas, Texas, on Thursday, 28 May 2026.

item.title: An unknown number of people killed in gas explosion at Dallas apartment building

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen explores how presidential contenders from both parties will need to grapple with ever-shifting AI issues ahead of 2028.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Gavin Newsom, left, and JD Vance
media:content.media:description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom; Vice President JD Vance.

item.title: AI is moving fast. 2028 hopefuls will be forced to adapt: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: An apparent “swatting” incident targeted Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett’s home Wednesday night, police in Virginia said Thursday.

media:content.media:text: U.S. Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett.
media:content.media:description: Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett and members of her family have been targeted before.

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett’s home targeted in apparent swatting incident

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: A 12-year-old boy drowned Wednesday during a class rafting trip in the Poconos region of Pennsylvania

media:content.media:text: Pierson Middle High School in Sag Harbor, N.Y.
media:content.media:description: Pierson Middle High School in Sag Harbor, N.Y.

item.title: Sixth grader dies during annual school rafting trip to the Poconos

item.dc:creator: Sara G. Miller
item.description: If additional Americans contract Ebola and need medical care, they will be sent to Europe rather than brought home to the U.S., senior administration officials said.

media:content.media:text: Workers load World Health Organization emergency supplies onto a United Nations plane
media:content.media:description: Workers load World Health Organization emergency supplies onto a United Nations plane in Nairobi, Kenya, on May 20.

item.title: Americans who get Ebola will go to Europe for treatment, not U.S., officials say

item.dc:creator: Berkeley Lovelace Jr.
item.description: A Food and Drug Administration advisory panel on Thursday recommended updating this fall’s Covid shots to target the XFG variant, a fast-growing strain nicknamed “stratus.”The recommendation — from the FDA’s Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee — is meant to help vaccine makers prepare shots for the fall and winter, when Covid infections typically rise

media:content.media:text: A flu and covid vaccine.
media:content.media:description: As of May 8, XFG and its descendants account for more than half of new infections in the U.S.

item.title: FDA panel recommends updating covid shots to target XFG strain

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins
Tom Winter
item.description: A central figure in sweeping gambling schemes involving pro and college basketball pleaded guilty Thursday to several charges stemming from two separate criminal cases

media:content.media:text: Marves Fairley.
media:content.media:description: Marves Fairley leaves Brooklyn federal court in New York City in December.

item.title: ‘Gambling guru’ pleads guilty to bribing players, buying inside information in two federal cases

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Alexis Boentes
item.description: Luis Raúl González-Pardo Rodríguez was sentenced for providing false information to U.S. immigration authorities last year when he applied for permanent residency.

media:content.media:text: Cuban pilot Luis Raul Gonzalez-Pardo Rodriguez in a MiG fighter jet.
media:content.media:description: Cuban pilot Luis Raul González-Pardo Rodríguez in a MiG fighter jet.

item.title: Cuban pilot listed in Raúl Castro indictment sentenced in immigration fraud case

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
Andrew Greif
item.description: These days, tennis fans can expect to see the Big Two, Italy’s Jannik Sinner and Spain’s Carlos Alcaraz, in the finals of pretty much every Grand Slam.

media:content.media:text: 2026 French Open - Day Five
media:content.media:description: Jannik Sinner during his loss to Juan Manuel Cerundolo at the French Open on Thursday.

media:content.media:text: An ill Jannik Sinner of Italy watched by a ball boy during his five-set loss against Juan Manuel Cerundolo at the French Open on May 28, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Jannik Sinner during his loss to Juan Manuel Cerundolo at the French Open on Thursday.

media:content.media:text: Jordan Staal of the Carolina Hurricanes scores a goal against Jakub Dobes of the Montréal Canadiens on May 27, 2026 in Montreal.
media:content.media:description: Jannik Sinner during his loss to Juan Manuel Cerundolo at the French Open on Thursday.

media:content.media:text: Esmir Bajraktarevic of Bosnia & Herzegovina scores a winning penalty
media:content.media:description: Jannik Sinner during his loss to Juan Manuel Cerundolo at the French Open on Thursday.

item.title: French Open field wide open, after Jannik Sinner’s surprise exit

item.dc:creator: Monica Alba
Sarah Dean
item.description: The White House has yet to release any results from President Donald Trump’s medical and dental exams at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center this week.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump.
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump during a cabinet meeting at the White House on May 27, 2026.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung At White House
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump in the Oval Office of the White House in August.

item.title: White House has yet to release health results from Trump’s doctor after latest checkup

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
Cory Fernandez
Bethany Heitman
item.description: I found the best methods and products for cleaning glasses, including reading glasses, sunglasses and more. Optometrists recommend the best cleaning products.

media:content.media:text: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.
media:content.media:description: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.

media:content.media:text: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.
media:content.media:description: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.

item.title: This is how you should actually be cleaning your glasses

item.dc:creator: Bianca Alvarez
item.description: Bar soaps work as effectively as body washes and I rounded up editor-approved picks from Dove, L’Occitane, Avène and more.

media:content.media:text: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.
media:content.media:description: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.

media:content.media:text: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.
media:content.media:description: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.

item.title: Bar soaps vs. body wash — which is best for your skin?

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: Sandals are notoriously not great for lots of walking. These podiatrists-approved sandals have built-in arch support and cushioning to keep you comfortable.

media:content.media:text: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.
media:content.media:description: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.

media:content.media:text: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.
media:content.media:description: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.

item.title: 6 podiatrist-approved sandals to comfortably walk in all summer

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: The Ninja Creami is one of the best ice cream makers, but are there better options? I tested it against KitchenAid, Cuisinart Fast Freeze and more to find out.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Cory Fernandez and Zoe Malin
media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Cory Fernandez and Zoe Malin
media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Ninja Creami on a granite table with the mixing paddle beside it.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: Cuisinart FastFreeze ice cream maker on a table.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: Light green KitchenAid Stand Mixer with ice cream maker attachment installed.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The GreenPan Frost sitting on a table with the lid beside it.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

media:content.media:text: The Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker on a table.
media:content.media:description: The Ninja Creami is also available in a deluxe version, which makes more ice cream and has more pre-sets.

item.title: I tested the Ninja Creami against five popular competitors, and the results shocked me
